discovery.run_summary: Risk-off tone in tech driven by a WSJ report that OpenAI is missing revenue/user targets, dragging the AI complex (ORCL -4%, AVGO -4.4%, TSM -3.2%, SMH -3%). Energy and staples bid on Iran/Hormuz oil spike near $100 and UAE OPEC exit. Mid-cap semis and AI-adjacent names showing sympathy selling worth examining for overshoots.

notable_index_moves: SMH -2.97% leading decline on OpenAI demand fears (WSJ)
XLE +1.67% on oil near $100, Iran tensions, UAE OPEC exit
XLP +0.92% defensive bid; XLRE +0.99% on rate softness
VIX only 17.78 (-1.3%) — selling is concentrated, not broad panic

discoveries.name: Lattice Semiconductor
discoveries.mechanism: Sympathy selling within semis on OpenAI/AI demand concerns, but Lattice (FPGA, edge/industrial/comms exposure) has limited direct AI training/hyperscaler revenue tie. Move tracks SMH but fundamentals less exposed than AVGO/ORCL.
discoveries.catalyst: WSJ OpenAI revenue miss report dragging entire SMH complex
research_pointers: Confirm LSCC's revenue mix — how much is data center/AI vs. industrial/auto/comms
Check if any LSCC-specific news (earnings date, guidance) is in window
Compare to prior AI-scare drawdowns and recovery pattern
discoveries.what_would_falsify: LSCC-specific negative news (guidance cut, customer loss, insider selling) or evidence of meaningful hyperscaler exposure

discoveries.name: Photronics
discoveries.mechanism: Photomask supplier swept up in semi sympathy selling, but volume only 1.1x average suggests it's drift, not conviction selling. PLAB business is mature foundry/display masks — minimal AI-capex correlation.
research_pointers: Verify no company-specific news; check earnings calendar
PLAB exposure to leading-edge AI logic vs. mature/display nodes
Historical correlation of PLAB to SMH sell-offs
discoveries.what_would_falsify: Discovery of PLAB-specific bad news or a meaningful customer concentration in AI-exposed foundries

discoveries.name: BridgeBio Pharma
discoveries.mechanism: 5.4x volume on a -6.5% move with no obvious sector catalyst (XBI only -1.4%) strongly implies stock-specific news — likely Attruby (acoramidis) launch metrics, competitor data, or label/payer development. Not in news feed provided.
research_pointers: Check for Attruby (ATTR-CM) script/launch data update or analyst note
Look for Alnylam/Pfizer competitive ATTR data readout
Check 8-K filings, conference presentations today
Review options activity for any leak signal
discoveries.what_would_falsify: Confirmed positive launch update would make this a clear opportunity; confirmed negative competitive readout would justify the move

discoveries.name: Align Technology
discoveries.mechanism: Down 9.2% over 5 days with elevated volume — looks like persistent selling pressure ahead of/around earnings. Not consistent with sector (XLV +0.23%).
discoveries.catalyst: Likely earnings-related positioning or pre-announcement; consumer discretionary dental softness possible
research_pointers: Confirm earnings date and consensus expectations
Check competitor (SmileDirect, Envista) commentary on dental demand
Review international/China case volume trends (key ALGN driver)
discoveries.what_would_falsify: Confirmation of guide-down or known negative pre-announcement

discoveries.name: Watsco
discoveries.mechanism: HVAC distributor down 4% on 1.9x volume with no apparent macro driver — XLI only -0.89%. Likely earnings reaction or guidance. Pool Corp (POOL, also industrial distribution) also down ~4%, suggesting either shared earnings day or sympathy in distribution sub-sector.
discoveries.catalyst: Probable earnings or sympathetic move with POOL
research_pointers: Check WSO earnings release today — margins, refrigerant transition (A2L) commentary
POOL/WSO earnings often viewed as housing-adjacent reads
Look at Carrier/Lennox/Trane reactions for HVAC channel signal
discoveries.what_would_falsify: Strong earnings with no guide-down would suggest overdone; weak earnings would justify

discoveries.name: Pool Corporation
discoveries.mechanism: Down 7.2% over 5 days, paired with WSO weakness — distribution/discretionary housing read deteriorating. Could be earnings or pre-announcement.
discoveries.catalyst: Possible earnings reaction or housing-channel weakness
research_pointers: Confirm POOL earnings date
Check pool/spa sub-sector (Hayward HAYW) for confirming weakness
Existing home sales / discretionary big-ticket data
discoveries.what_would_falsify: POOL-specific positive update or strong same-store sales data

discoveries.name: Advanced Energy Industries
discoveries.mechanism: Power conversion / semicap-adjacent — legitimate sympathy with semi capex narrative. However volume only 1.1x suggests passive/index-driven, not conviction. AEIS data center segment IS exposed to AI buildout, so move has fundamental basis but may overshoot if AI capex narrative reverses.
discoveries.catalyst: AI/data center capex de-rate via OpenAI WSJ story
research_pointers: AEIS data center revenue mix and customer concentration
Recent guidance commentary on hyperscaler orders
Compare reaction magnitude to peer Vertiv (VRT)
discoveries.what_would_falsify: Confirmation that hyperscaler capex commitments remain intact (e.g., MSFT/META capex guidance unchanged at upcoming earnings)

discoveries.name: Onto Innovation
discoveries.mechanism: Semicap metrology/inspection — legitimate AI-capex exposure (HBM/advanced packaging). Down with TSM/AVGO is rational, but low volume (1.1x) suggests not panic. Onto has high HBM/packaging leverage which is precisely the AI-driven segment.
research_pointers: ONTO HBM/advanced packaging exposure as % of revenue
Whether HBM demand from non-OpenAI customers (Anthropic, Meta, Google) offsets
Earnings date and any guide implications
discoveries.what_would_falsify: Independent HBM demand confirmation from SK Hynix/Micron earnings

catalyst_chains.primary_event: WSJ report OpenAI missing revenue/user targets
Iran war / UAE leaving OPEC / oil near $100

secondary_opportunities.relationship: input cost pressure (already announced $500M tariff refund offset)

discovery.no_signals_note: Most interesting unexplained mover is BBIO (-6.5% on 5.4x volume) — investigate first; clearly stock-specific news not in feed. AI-complex sympathy selling in mid-cap semis (LSCC, PLAB) merits scrutiny for overshoot vs. names with genuine AI capex exposure (AEIS, ONTO) where the move is more fundamentally justified.

ai_announcements.headline: Amazon offers new OpenAI products on AWS following Microsoft exclusivity end
ai_announcements.summary: AWS announced OpenAI model offerings including a new agent service, one day after OpenAI ended Microsoft exclusivity.

ai_announcements.headline: Google expands Pentagon AI access after Anthropic refusal
ai_announcements.summary: Google signed new DoD contract after Anthropic declined certain use cases (mass surveillance, autonomous weapons).

ai_announcements.headline: Red Hat's OpenClaw (Claude-related) container hardening for enterprise agent deployments
ai_announcements.summary: Tank OS containerizes OpenClaw AI agents for safer fleet deployment. Anthropic-ecosystem adjacent.

ai_analysis.no_signals_note: None of today's moving stocks (BBIO, PLAB, LSCC, WSO, ONTO, ALGN, BEAM, POOL, AEIS) have a plausible direct linkage to today's AI announcements. BBIO/BEAM/ALGN moves are healthcare-specific (likely earnings/clinical). PLAB/LSCC/ONTO/AEIS are semis — could reflect broader semi/AI capex sentiment but no announcement today plausibly drove a 4-6% selloff in these specific names; more likely earnings/guidance from semi peers or macro. WSO/POOL are industrial distributors with no AI announcement nexus today (likely earnings reactions, both report around this time). No actionable AI-driven mispricing signal in this dataset. Anthropic-related items (OpenClaw container, DoD refusal) flagged for bias-tracking but have no public-stock impact pathway.
